videos.title: From Sicily to the French Riviera | Nothing Went as Planned 🇮🇹🇫🇷 (#57)
videos.description:
✈️🇮🇹🇫🇷 We Thought We Were Going Home to Relax... We Couldn't Have Been More Wrong.

The plan sounded simple.
Leave Sicily.
Travel back to Sweden.
Take a few days off.
Relax.
Instead...
We somehow ended up dragging our entire lives across Europe.
😅
What started as a straightforward trip quickly turned into another adventure filled with missed expectations, heavy suitcases, long travel days, endless walking, spontaneous detours, and moments where we genuinely wondered what we were doing.
Along the way, we found ourselves exploring some of the most beautiful places on the Mediterranean coast.
🌴 The glamour of the French Riviera
⚓ The stunning peninsula of Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat
🇮🇹 The colorful streets of Sanremo
🍝 Incredible food
🌊 Spectacular sea views
🍷 Moments that reminded us why we love this lifestyle
Of course, no trip would be complete without a little chaos.
Or a lot.
There were train stations, airports, travel stress, aching feet, overloaded luggage, forgotten items, too much pasta, too little sleep, and at least one moment of...
"Wait... where are the passports?!"
😂
But that's life for us these days.
Living between Sweden and Sicily means that every journey becomes part travel documentary, part comedy show, and part survival challenge.
And honestly?
We wouldn't have it any other way.
❤️ Join us as we travel across Italy and France, chase beautiful views, enjoy incredible food, and somehow manage to survive another unforgettable adventure.
Because sometimes the best stories begin when your plans completely fall apart.
💛 In this episode:
🇮🇹 Leaving Sicily
🚄 Trains, airports & travel chaos
🇫🇷 Exploring the French Riviera
🌴 Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat
🌊 Mediterranean views
🍝 Amazing food
🧳 Living out of suitcases
😂 Travel mishaps and unexpected moments
❤️ Life between Sweden and Sicily
If you enjoy travel adventures, Mediterranean lifestyles, food, culture, and real-life travel chaos, you're in the right place.
Don't forget to LIKE, COMMENT and SUBSCRIBE to follow our adventures across Europe and beyond.
Ciao! 🇮🇹❤️🇫🇷

videos.title: This Could Be Your Life in Sicily… Would You Dare? 🇮🇹 (# 55)
videos.description:
What started as a dream turned into three years of dust, chaos… and something unforgettable.
Two Swedes. One house in Sicily. No turning back.

Along the way, we discovered something surprising — there are thousands of abandoned and affordable homes all over this island.
Whether you dream of a full renovation project… or a beautiful, move-in-ready home — Sicily has it all.
Quiet village life where everyone knows your name…
or vibrant cities like Cefalù, Catania, and Palermo.
Houses are everywhere.
It’s not about finding one — it’s about finding the one.
(We visited one. Just one. And that was it… somehow it worked out.)
🌅 And this… this could be your life too.
✨ And if you don’t feel like searching?
We might have something for you.
A friend of ours is now selling their newly renovated apartment in Termini Imerese —
around 100 sqm, space for 5 people, two bathrooms (one with shower), stunning views, and incredibly tasteful design.
A true hidden gem.
🔥 And today?
Back in Sweden we would celebrate Walpurgis Night — gathering around bonfires, singing, and welcoming spring.
But here in Sicily… we’re lighting the grill instead.
Different tradition. Same feeling.
A new life, a new rhythm… and maybe a new dream.
👇 So tell us — could you see yourself living like this?

videos.title: We Were Stranded in the Mountains... Then an Angel Arrived 🇮🇹❤️(#53)
videos.description:
🇮🇹 What started as a wonderful day exploring Sicily quickly turned into one of the most frightening experiences we've had since moving here.
After visiting Corleone and enjoying the breathtaking landscapes of inland Sicily, everything suddenly changed.
Far from towns, far from help, and surrounded by mountains, we found ourselves stranded in a situation that became more serious with every passing minute.
For most people, it would have been stressful.
For Andreas, who has Type 1 diabetes, it became something much more alarming.
When you're dependent on insulin and careful planning every day, unexpected situations can quickly become dangerous. As the hours passed and options seemed limited, we realized just how vulnerable we really were.
This could have ended very differently.
But sometimes, when things look darkest, someone appears who restores your faith in humanity.
His name was Antonio.
A complete stranger who stepped in when we needed help the most.
An angel in Sicily.
❤️
His kindness, generosity, and willingness to help reminded us why we fell in love with this island in the first place. Sicily is not just about beautiful landscapes, historic towns, and incredible food.
It's about the people.
The moments of unexpected kindness.
The human connections that stay with you long after the day is over.
And after a day like this, there was only one way to celebrate.
Pizza.
🍕✨
Back home in Termini Imerese, we visited one of our favorite places: Tocca a Tia.
More than just a restaurant or pizzeria, Tocca a Tia represents something special. It combines outstanding Sicilian food with values that matter: inclusion, community, local identity, and a genuine passion for bringing people together.
Using quality local ingredients and supporting social engagement within the community, it has become a place that reflects the very best of modern Sicily.
A place where food brings people together.
A place where strangers become friends.
And after the day we'd just experienced, it felt like the perfect ending.
Join us for a journey through the mountains of Sicily, an unexpected crisis, an unforgettable act of kindness, and a reminder that sometimes the most important part of travel isn't the places you visit...
It's the people you meet along the way.
💛 Have you ever met a stranger who changed your day—or even your life?
Tell us your story in the comments.

videos.title: Inside Corleone: The Story of the Sicilian Mafia (#51)
videos.description:
🇮🇹 Corleone.
For many people, the name immediately brings one thing to mind:
The Mafia.
For decades, this small Sicilian town became synonymous with power, fear, violence, and the rise of Cosa Nostra. It was here that some of the most notorious Mafia bosses built their influence, leaving a legacy that would shape Sicily's history and capture the world's imagination through books, films, and headlines.
In this episode, we travel to Corleone to uncover the real story behind the myths.
We explore the town's complex history, learn about the rise of the Sicilian Mafia, and reflect on the devastating impact organized crime had on communities across Sicily.
But this is not just a story about darkness.
It is also a story about courage.
We pay tribute to anti-Mafia heroes Giovanni Falcone and Paolo Borsellino, whose determination and sacrifice helped challenge the culture of fear and silence. Their legacy continues to inspire Sicily today and reminds us that even the most powerful systems can be confronted by those brave enough to stand up for what is right.
Yet what surprised us most was discovering a completely different side of Corleone.
Beyond the headlines and history lies a region of extraordinary beauty.
Rolling hills.
Ancient traditions.
Warm hospitality.
And a slower, more authentic Sicily that many visitors never get to see.
During our stay, we checked into Il Bevaio di Corleone, a charming agriturismo surrounded by nature, where we spent the night in a cozy bungalow and enjoyed a wonderful Sicilian dinner under the stars.
It was the perfect reminder that Corleone today is not defined by its past.
It is a place of resilience, culture, breathtaking landscapes, and people determined to write a new chapter for their community.
Join us as we discover the real Corleone — beyond the stereotypes, beyond the movies, and beyond the Mafia.
This is Corleone today.
And you're invited to come along.
💛 If you enjoy authentic Sicily, hidden stories, local culture, and discovering places beyond the tourist trail, don't forget to LIKE, COMMENT, and SUBSCRIBE.

When we moved from Sweden to a small town in Sicily, we dreamed of a simpler life.
Less stress.
More sunshine.
Fresh food.
And time to enjoy the little things.
In this episode, we're taking you along for a glimpse of what everyday life really looks like in our corner of Sicily.

Steep streets, endless staircases, and dramatic elevation changes mean every errand comes with breathtaking views—and a little extra cardio.
As we wander through the town, you'll experience the sights, sounds, flavors, and atmosphere that made us fall in love with Sicily in the first place.
No tourist attractions.

Just real life.
Slow mornings.

After weeks away, we're finally back in Sicily.
But before we even have time to unpack, Sicily has other plans.
Linda arrives in Termini Imerese and immediately finds herself in the middle of one of the island's most spectacular traditions: Carnevale Termitano, often considered the oldest Carnival in Sicily.
The streets explode with music, giant floats, costumes, laughter, and thousands of people celebrating a tradition that has lived on for generations.
But as always, real life has a way of interrupting the party...
😅 Linda takes a nasty fall shortly after arriving, ends up bruised and battered, and somehow manages to destroy her phone in the process.
✈️ Meanwhile, Andreas returns from a work trip to Germany and jumps straight back into renovation mode.
And that's when things get serious.
After experiencing several small electrical fires in our house over the past year, Andreas explains one of the biggest challenges of renovating old Sicilian homes: how do you install modern electrical systems without destroying historic walls, ceilings, and character?
⚡ You'll see practical solutions, renovation realities, and important lessons that every owner of an old house should know.
🎨 At the same time, Linda returns to one of her favorite projects—bringing creativity back by painting a brand-new mural in our stairwell, adding another personal touch to our ever-evolving Sicilian home.
This episode has a little bit of everything:
🎭 Carnival celebrations
🏡 Historic house renovation
⚡ Electrical challenges and safety
🎨 Art and creativity
😅 Travel mishaps
❤️ Everyday life in Sicily
Because that's what life here really looks like.
One moment you're dancing at a 150-year-old Carnival.
The next you're discussing electrical fires and painting walls.
And honestly...
We wouldn't want it any other way.
💛 If you enjoy authentic Sicily, renovation reality, travel adventures, and the occasional disaster, you're in the right place.

Have you ever arrived somewhere at exactly the right moment...
Or had a trip go completely off the rails?
We'd love to hear your story.

videos.title: Palermo: The City People Warn You About (And You Might Love Anyway) (#47)
videos.description:
🇮🇹 Palermo is a city that refuses to be ignored.
Some visitors arrive, see the traffic, hear the horns, and immediately decide they never want to come back.
Others fall in love.
With the energy.
With the imperfections.
With the history.
With the feeling that this city is alive in a way few places still are.
In this episode, we explore both sides of Palermo.
We begin in the city's famously chaotic traffic, where scooters, cars, pedestrians, and pure Sicilian determination somehow coexist in organized madness. First impressions can be overwhelming—but if you stay a little longer, Palermo slowly reveals its secrets.
🏛️ We visit the magnificent Teatro Massimo, one of Europe's most impressive opera houses.
⛪ We wander among grand cathedrals and historic architecture shaped by centuries of different cultures and empires.
🎶 We stumble upon a spontaneous opera performance in a city square, reminding us that in Palermo, art isn't confined to museums or concert halls—it lives in the streets.
🌊 And when the city becomes too intense, we escape to the crystal-clear waters of Mondello, Palermo's beloved beach paradise, where locals and visitors alike come to slow down and breathe.
🍝 Finally, we end the day the way every Sicilian day should end: around a table filled with incredible food, laughter, and conversation.
Because if there's one thing that unites everyone in Palermo, it's the food.
This isn't a guidebook version of Sicily.
This is Palermo as it really is.
Beautiful.
Messy.
Historic.
Modern.
Frustrating.
Magical.
And completely unforgettable.

Are you Team "Never Again" or Team "I Love Palermo"?

videos.title: Two and a Half Years in Sicily | Our Italian life and house (#46)
videos.description:
🇮🇹 Two and a Half Years Ago, We Changed Everything.
At an age when many people are thinking about slowing down, we did the opposite.
We packed up our lives, left Sweden behind, and moved to Sicily with no real plan, no renovation experience, and absolutely no idea what we were getting ourselves into.
What started as a dream quickly became an adventure.
An adventure filled with sunshine, sea views, unexpected challenges, incredible friendships, endless cups of espresso, and more renovation dust than we ever thought possible. ☕🏡🌊
This film is our love letter to Sicily.
To the island that captured our hearts.
To the mornings filled with church bells and fresh pastries.
To the warm Mediterranean evenings where time seems to stand still.
To the colorful markets, the ancient architecture, the simple but unforgettable food, and the traditions that still thrive here today.
And of course...
To the olive harvest.
The season when families, friends, and generations come together to transform months of hard work into liquid gold. 🫒✨
When we first arrived, we bought the very first house we viewed.
Yes... really.
Most people spend months searching.
We spent about an hour. 😅
We fell in love instantly with its painted ceilings, original floors, hidden character, and endless potential.
Since then, we've spent two and a half years restoring, renovating, learning, making mistakes, and slowly transforming an old Sicilian house into a home.
A place where friends gather.
A place where family visits.
A place where dreams continue to grow.
This is not a story about perfection.
It's a story about taking a chance.
About finding beauty in imperfection.
About choosing experiences over comfort.
And about discovering that sometimes the best things in life begin with a single leap of faith.
If you've ever dreamed of starting over, moving abroad, restoring an old house, living slower, eating better, and embracing life with all five senses fully awake...
Welcome to Sicily.
💛 Tell us in the comments:
If you arrived in Sicily tomorrow, what would you do first?
☕ Enjoy an espresso in a sunlit piazza
🍦 Eat a giant pistachio gelato
🌊 Dive into the Mediterranean Sea
We'd love to know.
And if you enjoy our journey, please LIKE, SUBSCRIBE, and SHARE. It helps us continue this adventure more than you can imagine.
🇮🇹 Benvenuti in Sicilia.

videos.title: We Made an Offer on Our Dream Olive Farm... Now What? 🫒 (#45)
videos.description:
We started the process of buying land in Sicily back in October. It's now January... and we STILL haven't signed anything. 😅

In this video, we're sharing the REAL process of buying property in Italy - the good, the bad, and the missing 1983 document that's holding everything up.

If you're dreaming of buying an olive grove, vineyard, or farmhouse in Sicily (or anywhere in Italy), watch this first. It might save you months of frustration and thousands of euros.

🫒 THE PROPERTY:
• 15,000 m² (3.7 acres) of land
• ~150 olive trees
• Vineyard
• 2 structures
• 70,000 liter water tank with natural source

📋 WHAT WE COVER:
0:00 - The situation (3 months and counting...)
0:45 - The property tour
2:00 - The official buying process in Italy
4:30 - All the people involved (notaio, geometra, comune...)
6:00 - THE 1983 PROBLEM (the missing document)
9:00 - The tax surprise nobody warned us about
10:30 - Where we are now
11:30 - 6 lessons we've learned (so far)

🎓 KEY LESSONS:
1. NEVER sign the compromesso before ALL paperwork is verified
2. Budget way more time than you think
3. Get a good team (notaio, geometra, agent)
4. Understand exactly how taxes are calculated
5. Patience is not optional in Italy
6. Be emotionally prepared to walk away

videos.title: -9°C in Sweden... But Our Hearts Are Already in Sicily 🇸🇪❄️➡️🇮🇹☀️ (#44)
videos.description:
❄️🇸🇪 While Sweden Freezes, We Dream of Sicily ☀️🇮🇹
Winter has truly arrived in Sweden.
With temperatures dropping to -9°C, frozen landscapes, and snow-covered gardens, we're enjoying everything that makes a Scandinavian winter magical. Cozy family moments, outdoor cooking, crackling fires, and time spent with loved ones remind us why we will always cherish our Swedish roots.
But while we're embracing the cold, our thoughts keep drifting south...
To Sicily.
To sunshine, olive trees, mountain views, and the life we've built in our little corner of Italy.
As we prepare for our return, we're also looking ahead to what could be the most exciting year of our Sicilian adventure so far. New renovation projects are waiting, plans are taking shape, and our dream of owning an olive grove feels closer than ever.
In this episode, we slow down and enjoy the simple pleasures of winter while sharing what's happening behind the scenes and what's waiting for us when we return to Sicily.
🌲 A snowy Swedish winter
🔥 Family time around the fire
🍡 Marshmallow barbecue in freezing temperatures
🏡 Updates on our historic Sicilian home
🫒 The latest news about our olive grove dream
🇮🇹 Preparations for our return to Sicily
✨ Big plans for the year ahead
Living between two countries is a strange and beautiful experience. One moment we're surrounded by snow and winter darkness, the next we're dreaming of Mediterranean sunshine and life among the olive trees.
If you've ever dreamed of moving abroad, creating a new life in another country, or simply following your heart toward something bigger, we think you'll enjoy this episode.
The adventure is far from over.
In fact, it feels like it's only just beginning.
💛 Thank you for following our journey and being part of our story.
Don't forget to LIKE, COMMENT and SUBSCRIBE to join us as we continue building our dream life in Sicily.
Ciao for now – we'll see you back under the Sicilian sun very soon! ☀️🇮🇹

videos.title: We Missed Sicily More Than We Expected 🇮🇹❤️ (#43)
videos.description:
🎄🇸🇪🇮🇹 From a Swedish Winter Wonderland Back to Our Sicilian Dream
After spending a magical Christmas and New Year in Sweden surrounded by family, friends, snow, and traditions, it's finally time to begin the next chapter of our adventure.
For a few precious weeks, we exchanged Sicilian sunshine for snowy landscapes, cozy evenings, traditional Swedish Christmas celebrations, and quality time with the people we love most. We decorated the Christmas tree, enjoyed festive buffets, introduced our Italian friends to Swedish holiday traditions, and welcomed the New Year with laughter, food, and unforgettable memories.
But while Sweden gave us a beautiful holiday season, our hearts never stopped thinking about Sicily.
We miss the sunshine.
We miss the sea.
We miss the sound of life in Termini Imerese.
We miss our morning walks along the beach.
And we miss Belvedere, the place that has become our second home.
As 2025 begins, we're ready to return to Sicily and continue the journey that started with a dream and a house in a small Sicilian town.
In this episode, we look back on our Swedish holiday celebrations and share why we're so excited to head back south for another year filled with renovations, adventures, surprises, and life in beautiful Sicily.
💛 In this episode:
🎄 Decorating the Christmas tree
💃 Teaching Swedish traditions to our Italian friends

❄️ The arrival of snow
🐱 Our cat discovers television

The adventure continues...
And we would love for you to come along.

videos.title: From House Owners to Olive Farmers? 🫒 Our Next Sicilian Adventure (#42)
videos.description:
🫒🇮🇹 We Just Made an Offer on Our Dream Olive Farm in Sicily!
Two years ago, we took a leap of faith and bought a house in Sicily.
Now, we're standing at the edge of an even bigger dream.
For a long time, Linda has dreamed of having her own piece of land—a place where we can grow food, harvest olives, make wine, cook outdoors, and create unforgettable experiences surrounded by nature.
After months of searching, we may have finally found it.
Nestled in a peaceful Sicilian valley lies a breathtaking 15,000-square-meter property that feels almost too good to be true.
🌿 Around 150 olive trees
🍇 A vineyard producing its own wine
🍊 Orange, lemon, fig, peach, walnut, and other fruit trees
💧 A massive 70,000-liter water reservoir with a complete irrigation system
🏔️ Stunning mountain views
🌊 And on clear days... even a glimpse of the sea
In this episode, we take you with us as we explore the property, harvest olives, visit the olive mill, press our own extra virgin olive oil, and imagine what the future could look like.
Could this become the next chapter of our Sicilian adventure?
We're dreaming about so much more than just owning land.
Imagine:
✨ Olive harvest weekends
✨ Farm-to-table dinners
✨ Wine tastings
✨ Outdoor cooking experiences
✨ Glamping under the Sicilian stars
✨ A place where friends, family, and guests can experience authentic Sicily
We've already made our offer.
Now comes the hardest part...
Waiting.
Will this incredible property become ours?
Join us as we take another step toward building the life we've always dreamed of in Sicily.
🤞 Fingers crossed.

videos.title: We Opened the Boxes... And Couldn't Believe It ❤️📦 (#41)
videos.description:
📖 After years of writing, dreaming, editing, doubting, rewriting, and believing... the day finally arrived.
Linda's new psychological thriller, IMAGO, has officially been released.
When the first boxes of freshly printed books arrived at our house, everything suddenly became real. What had lived for years as an idea, then a manuscript, and finally a finished novel, was now something we could hold in our hands.

This episode takes you behind the scenes of one of the biggest milestones of our lives.
You'll follow us through the emotional days leading up to the launch as we prepare handcrafted gift boxes for guests, decorate with olive branches from Sicily, bake traditional butterfly-shaped pastries, and put the finishing touches on a celebration that has been years in the making.
But this isn't just a story about publishing a book.
It's a story about courage.
About following a dream despite uncertainty.
About creating something from nothing and sharing it with the world.
You'll join us at the release party in Katrineholm, where family, friends, readers, and supporters gather to celebrate IMAGO's arrival. There are speeches, laughter, emotions, and moments we'll never forget.
❤️ In this episode:
📦 The arrival of the very first IMAGO books
🎁 Handcrafted launch gifts inspired by Sicily
🦋 Traditional butterfly pastries baked for the celebration
🎉 Behind the scenes of the release party
🗣️ Speeches from Andreas and Linda
📖 The story behind IMAGO and what inspired it
❤️ Family, friends, and readers celebrating together
IMAGO is a psychological thriller that explores identity, relationships, perception, and transformation. Parts of the story are inspired by the landscapes, atmosphere, and mysteries of Sicily — a place that has become an important part of our lives.
Whether you're a reader, a writer, a dreamer, or simply someone who loves seeing people pursue their passion, we hope this episode inspires you.
Because sometimes the biggest adventures don't begin with a plane ticket.
Sometimes they begin with a blank page.
📚 IMAGO is available now.
Thank you for being part of this journey.
⸻
💛 Don't forget to LIKE, COMMENT and SUBSCRIBE to follow our adventures in Sicily, our creative projects, and everything that happens next.
From Sicily to Sweden, from dream to reality — thank you for being here.

videos.title: The Most Beautiful Drive in Europe? 🇮🇹🏔️🇦🇹" (#40)
videos.description:
🚙🇮🇹🇦🇹 From Sicily to Sweden – One Incredible View at a Time
Our journey across Europe continues, and today we're leaving Italy behind as we head north toward Austria on one of the most spectacular drives we've ever experienced.
The route along the A22 motorway takes us through breathtaking mountain landscapes, winding valleys, and views that seem almost too beautiful to be real. Every turn reveals another postcard-perfect scene, reminding us why we love traveling by car and exploring Europe at our own pace.
But not everything goes according to plan...
After spending the night in a historic castle, we wake up feeling less like royalty and more like we've been sleeping on medieval torture devices! 😅 With aching backs and slightly tired bodies, we hit the road once again, determined to continue our adventure.
Along the way, we share the reality of life on a long-distance road trip:
🚗 Endless kilometers across Europe
🏰 Unexpected castle stays
🏔️ Jaw-dropping Alpine scenery
☕ Much-needed coffee breaks
😄 Travel mishaps and plenty of laughter

This journey isn't just about getting from Sicily to Sweden. It's about embracing every moment along the way—the good, the uncomfortable, and the unforgettable.
If you've ever dreamed of driving through Europe, exploring hidden places, and experiencing life beyond the typical tourist routes, this episode is for you.
Come along as we continue our epic road trip north and discover why sometimes the journey becomes the destination.
💛 Don't forget to LIKE, COMMENT and SUBSCRIBE to follow our adventure from Sicily to Sweden and back again.
Benvenuti and welcome aboard!
⸻
🕒 In this episode:
🏰 A night in a historic castle
😅 The consequences of sleeping in a historic castle
🚙 Driving north on the A22
🏔️ Incredible views of the Alps
☕ Road trip stops and travel reflections
🇦🇹 Arriving closer to Austria

videos.title: Andreas Gets a Tattoo | Epic Roadtrip to Sweden! (Palermo → Genova Ferry Adventure) (#39)
videos.description:
🇮🇹➡️🇸🇪 The journey home begins!
In this episode, Andreas starts the day by getting some fresh ink before we leave Sicily behind and begin our long road trip back to Sweden for Christmas.
With the tattoo finished and the holiday spirit in full swing, it's time for one last Sicilian tradition before departure: Glögg, packing, last-minute preparations, and saying goodbye to our beloved home in Sicily.
Then the real adventure begins.
We load the car, gather our olive oil, wine, Christmas gifts, and of course our brave travel companion 🐾, and make our way to the port of Palermo to board the ferry to Genova.
Join us as we take you behind the scenes of life on the road:
⛴️ Life aboard the ferry
🍽️ A huge lunch with Mediterranean views
🎄 Christmas preparations far from home
🚗 The first stage of our epic journey across Europe
🐱 How our cat handled the adventure
This episode is all about transitions — leaving one home behind while heading towards another. It's a mix of excitement, nostalgia, travel chaos, and the anticipation of spending Christmas with family in Sweden.
If you've ever wondered what it's like to live between two countries, travel thousands of kilometers with a car full of belongings, or simply enjoy following our Sicilian adventure, you'll love this episode.
And don't worry... this is only the beginning.
The next episode includes even more road trip adventures, unexpected moments, and the final stretch of our journey north.

videos.title: We Climbed Monreale… and Andreas Nearly Fainted! 😂 | Sarti Spritz Ending (#35)
videos.description:
Our Sicilian adventure continues — and this time we took on the legendary Monreale Monastery overlooking Palermo! 🇮🇹🌄 What started as a beautiful cultural visit quickly turned into an unforgettable (and hilarious!) moment… because Andreas massively underestimated his fear of heights. 😅

Join us as we climb higher and higher through the ancient monastery terraces, taking in the breathtaking views — while Andreas tries not to faint on camera 😂. His wife, calm as always, guides herself along as they make it all the way to the top (because of course they do — she always go ALL the way up!).

And after surviving Monreale’s epic heights?
We ended the day the only proper way you should in Sicily: Sarti Spritz with our amazing friends Marina and Patrik. 🍹✨ Good vibes, great laughs, and memories we’ll never forget.

If you love travel stories, raw reactions, good food, and authentic moments from real people exploring the world — this episode is for you. ❤️

videos.title: Italian Gems |Hidden Thermal Spot Found in the Sicilian mountains (#33)
videos.description:
Secret places in Sicily
Fisherman Invades Our Kitchen. Hidden Thermal Spot & Augustali Vineyard Tour in Sicily. Experience one of our most unforgettable Sicilian weeks yet! 🇮🇹✨ It begins with a surprise visit from our neighbor — a fisherman who walked straight into our kitchen and cooked the freshest Mediterranean catch you can imagine! The flavors, the chaos, the laughter… pure Sicilian magic.

After the feast, we stumbled upon a hidden natural thermal spring in the mountains near Termini Imerese — a true local secret far from tourist routes. And to complete the adventure, we toured the beautiful Augustali Vineyard in Partinico, tasting incredible organic wines and learning about sustainable Sicilian winemaking.

📍 In This Episode:
• Fresh Mediterranean fish cooked by a local fisherman
• A secret mountain thermal spot near Termini Imerese

videos.title: Fresh Extra Virgin Olive Oil in Sicily | From Tree to Bottle (#32)
videos.description:
220 kg of Olives. Fresh Extra Virgin Olive Oil in Sicily. From Tree to Bottle. Ever wondered how real extra virgin olive oil is made? 🌿 Join us in Trabia, Sicily, as we harvest 220 kg of olives with friends and follow the entire process — from tree to bottle! 🇮🇹✨

In this video, you’ll experience every step inside a traditional frantoio (olive mill): sorting, washing, crushing, malaxation, centrifugation, and that magical first pour of green-gold oil. 💚

We’ll share the tradition, technology, and taste behind one of Italy’s most iconic foods — and why freshness and quick processing make all the difference in quality, flavor, and health benefits.

💛 In This Video, You’ll See:
• Hand-picking 220 kg of Sicilian olives 🫒
• Visiting a real frantoio for cold-pressed extraction
• The moment the first oil appears!
• Bottling and tasting the freshest olive oil ever
• Why we bought 20 liters to take home
• Local food culture, friendship, and pure Sicily vibes

videos.title: Exploring Donnafugata Castle | “Il Gattopardo” , Sicilian Splendor & Local Cuisine (#25)
videos.description:
Benvenuti! 
In this episode we take you on a journey into the heart of Sicily’s aristocratic past, through the majestic Castello di Donnafugata, famed as one of the filming locations for Netflix’s new adaptation of Il Gattopardo (“The Leopard”).

1️⃣ The Castle & Its Legacy
The Castello di Donnafugata lies about 15 km from Ragusa in southeastern Sicily. 
Although the site has medieval roots, the building we see today was reshaped mostly in the late 19th century in neoclassical and neo-Gothic styles under the noble family Arezzo de Spuches. 
It houses over 120 rooms (about 20 are open to visitors) with period furnishings, decorative stucchi, trompe-l’oeil paintings, a “music room,” a grand hall of coats of arms, and more. 

Surrounding the castle is an 8-hectare park and garden, featuring a mix of French and English garden styles, a labyrinth built with local stone, grottoes, surprise water features (once playful traps), and a large variety of Mediterranean flora — more than 1,500 species. 
Visiting the castle is like stepping into another era — you can almost hear corsets rustle, candlelight flicker, and noble footsteps echoing in the corridors.
Ragusa and the Hyblean countryside (Sud-Est Sicilia) form the backdrop for our exploration: rolling hills, dry-stone walls, olive and carob trees, sun-bleached fields. 
The region is steeped in Baroque architecture, ancient traditions, strong local identity, and the interplay between rich landowners and rural communities.
This part of Sicily is less touristy (compared to Palermo or Taormina), which means more authenticity: local food, local stories, and real daily life.

After the castle tour, we immerse ourselves in the region’s flavors: gnocchi al forno, local wines, and genuine Sicilian hospitality at a neighborhood trattoria.

Il Gattopardo

 / The Netflix Series

Il Gattopardo (The Leopard) is a historical drama miniseries on Netflix, released on March 5, 2025. It’s based on Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa’s classic novel. 
The series tells the story of Don Fabrizio Corbera, Prince of Salina, and his noble family in 19th-century Sicily, as the island faces the seismic changes brought by Italy’s unification (the Risorgimento). 
Central themes: decline of aristocracy, social upheaval, tension between tradition and progress, love, betrayal, identity.
The series was filmed in multiple locations — Palermo (noble palaces, historic quarters), Catania, Syracuse, and interiors in Rome & Turin — all chosen to evoke the elegance and turmoil of that era. 

Donnafugata is treated as the summer residence / retreat of the Salina family in the show. While “Donnafugata” is partly fictional in the novel, the show’s creators used real Sicilian estates and landscapes to bring it to life.

videos.description:
Sicily—a land where every corner tells a story. From the creamy delight of world-famous gelato to the vibrant traditions of the Madonna della Catena festival, this journey dives deep into the flavors, faith, and heart of Sicilian culture.

🍦 Gelato Heaven in Sicily
What’s a trip to Sicily without gelato? From indulgent Nutella scoops to rich pistachio and refreshing fruit blends, each flavor is a taste of the island’s soul.

🎉 Madonna della Catena Festival
Join us in celebrating one of Sicily’s most cherished traditions. With lively processions, music, food, and community spirit, this annual festival shows the warmth and joy of Sicilian life.

❤️ Why Sicily Steals the Heart
Sicily is more than landscapes and food—it’s connection, simplicity, and timeless beauty. Whether you’re wandering historic streets, sharing laughs with locals, or savoring a ripe peach under the sun, Sicily will leave an unforgettable imprint on you.

videos.title: HELP! The facade is falling down on our Italian house! (#11)
videos.description:
Our 100-year-old house is desperately in need of a new façade. The walls are crumbling, cracks are appearing, and time has certainly left its mark. We know the work has to be done—but the big question is: can we actually afford it, and are we ready for the challenge?
Renovating an old Sicilian house is never as simple as it seems. Every project uncovers new surprises, unexpected costs, and difficult decisions. Yet this house has captured our hearts, and we're determined to give it the care it deserves.
To find inspiration, we travel to Agrigento, one of Sicily's most historic and fascinating places. Walking through its ancient streets, surrounded by buildings that have stood proudly for centuries, we're reminded of the incredible craftsmanship and resilience that define this island.
From the magnificent architecture of the old city to the world-famous Valley of the Temples, Agrigento is a place where history comes alive. If these ancient structures have survived for thousands of years, perhaps there's hope for our little house too.
Join us as we face one of the biggest renovation decisions yet, explore one of Sicily's greatest treasures, and continue our adventure of restoring a piece of Sicilian history.
Will we manage to save our crumbling façade, or is this project bigger than we imagined?
Come along and find out.

videos.description:
Should we renovate everything from scratch... or simply give it a stylish makeover?

In this episode, it's finally time to tackle our bedroom. The room has plenty of character, but it also needs some love. The big question is: do we invest in a complete renovation or work with what we already have and give it a fresh new look?
To help bring our vision to life, we head to Catania in search of inspiration and furniture. But should we buy brand-new pieces or embrace the charm of Sicily and hunt for unique vintage finds?
As always, every decision comes with challenges, compromises, and a little bit of adventure. Join us as we transform another part of our Sicilian home and try to create a bedroom that feels both beautiful and true to the character of the house.
Will we find exactly what we're looking for? Or will Sicily surprise us once again?
Come along for another step in our renovation journey and discover how we're turning an old Sicilian house into our dream home.

videos.title: REAL ARTISAN PIZZA made by our Sicilian neighbour. (#6)
videos.description:
This is REAL pizza dough.
The kind that takes patience, tradition, and generations of knowledge to perfect. The kind that Italians grow up with and instantly recognize. Crispy on the outside, soft and airy on the inside—exactly how authentic Italian pizza should look, smell, and taste.
Our wonderful neighbor Cristina invited me, Linda, into her kitchen for a private pizza-making lesson. No shortcuts, no secrets kept hidden—just genuine Sicilian know-how passed from one friend to another.
In this episode, you'll learn the techniques, tips, and traditions behind authentic Italian pizza dough as Cristina shares her family methods with us. From mixing and kneading to shaping and baking, you'll see every step of the process.
And of course, we'll taste the results!
So grab a seat at the table and join us for a true Sicilian experience, where food is more than a meal—it's a way of bringing people together.
Welcome to our little pizza school in Sicily. 🍕🇮🇹
